title: woodsia scopulina subsp. scopulina
intro: Woodsia scopulina subsp. scopulina, also known as Rock Cap Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Woodsia, native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Woodsia scopulina subsp. scopulina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system function, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Woodsia scopulina subsp. scopulina can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections.
